zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt於1336年1月13日出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾斯托克 阿爾班尼時，他的父親William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros51歲，母親Margery Badlesmere31歲。他於1363年4月12日與Beatrice Stafford在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1364年登記服兵役。他於1367年被列為office of the joint warden of the west march of scotland。他去世於1384年6月8日在英國林可爾史爾武分頓享年48歲，埋葬在英國越可爾爾姆斯利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt于1336年1月13日出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾斯托克 阿爾班尼时，他的父亲William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros51岁，母亲Margery Badlesmere31岁。他于1363年4月12日与Beatrice Stafford在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1364年登记服兵役。他于1367年被列为office of the joint warden of the west march of scotland。他去世于1384年6月8日在英国林可爾史爾武分頓享年48岁，埋葬在英国越可爾爾姆斯利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt was born on 13 January 1336, in Stoke Albany, Northamptonshire, England, his father, William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros, was 51 and his mother, Margery Badlesmere, was 31. He married Beatrice Stafford on 12 April 1363, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He registered for military service in 1364. In 1367, at the age of 31, his occupation is listed as office of the joint warden of the west march of scotland. He died on 8 June 1384, in Uffington, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 48, and was buried in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt est né le 13 janvier 1336, à Stoke Albany, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Margery Badlesmere, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Beatrice Stafford le 12 avril 1363, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1364. En 1367, à l’âge de 31 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est office of the joint warden of the west march of scotland. Il est décédé le 8 juin 1384, à Uffington, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterré à Helmsley, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.Helmsley, North Yorkshire, England: Helmsley, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt am 13. Januar 1336 in Stoke Albany, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros 51 und seine Mutter Margery Badlesmere 31. Er hat am 12. April 1363 in England, United Kingdom Beatrice Stafford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er hat sich 1364 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1367, als er 31 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als office of the joint warden of the west march of scotland aufgeführt. Er starb am 8. Juni 1384 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Uffington, Lincolnshire, England und wurde in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt è nato il il 13 gennaio 1336 nel Stoke Albany, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros, aveva 51 anni e il suo madre, Margery Badlesmere, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Beatrice Stafford il 12 aprile 1363, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1364. Nel 1367, all'età di 31 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come office of the joint warden of the west march of scotland. Morì il 8 giugno 1384, nel Uffington, Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolto a Helmsley, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Ros, Kntが1336年1月13日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアストク アルバニーで生まれた時，父William de Ros 2nd Baron de Rosは51歳，母Margery Badlesmereは31歳であった。1363年4月12日にイギリスイギリスでBeatrice Staffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1364年に兵役登録をした。1367年，31歳の時，職業はoffice of the joint warden of the west march of scotlandと記録されている。彼は1384年6月8日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアウフィントンで48歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアヘルムスリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt(이)가 1336년 1월 13일 영국 노르섬프토실르 스토크 알바니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros(은)는 51세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery Badlesmere(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1363년 4월 12일 영국 잉글랜드에서 Beatrice Stafford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1364년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1367년, 31세의 나이로 그의 직업은 office of the joint warden of the west march of scotland이었다. 그는 1384년 6월 8일 영국 링코실르 핀그턴에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 헬슬리에 묻혔다.

pt.13 January 1336–8 June 1384: 13 de janeiro de 1336–8 de junho de 1384
pt.Helmsley, North Yorkshire, England: Helmsley, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt nasceu em 13 de janeiro de 1336, em Stoke Albany, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Margery Badlesmere, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Beatrice Stafford em 12 de abril de 1363, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1364. Em 1367, com 31 anos, sua profissão aparece como office of the joint warden of the west march of scotland. Ele faleceu em 8 de junho de 1384, em Uffington, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 48 anos, e foi sepultado em Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ro.Helmsley, North Yorkshire, England: Helmsley, North Yorkshire, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt родился 13 января 1336 г. в местности сток албани, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Margery Badlesmere, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrice Stafford 12 апреля 1363 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. В 1364 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1367 году, в возрасте 31 год, работал по профессии office of the joint warden of the west march of scotland. Он умер 8 июня 1384 г. в местности уффингтон, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности хелмсли, еркшир, Англия.

es.13 January 1336–8 June 1384: 13 de enero de 1336–8 de junio de 1384
es.Helmsley, North Yorkshire, England: Helmsley, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt nació el 13 de enero de 1336, en Stoke Albany, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Ros 2nd Baron de Ros, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Margery Badlesmere, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Beatrice Stafford el 12 de abril de 1363, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1364. En 1367, a la edad de 31 años, su ocupación registrada era office of the joint warden of the west march of scotland. Murió el 8 de junio de 1384, en Uffington, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultado en Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
